article_title: Physical separation between liquor retailer and other businesses
body: This change appliesonlyto the following licences: Liquor retail licensees were previously required to physically separate their stores from other businesses using floor-to-ceiling walls. In response to licensee feedback, the Liquor Control and Licensing Regulation has been amended to allow Licensee Retail Stores and Wine Stores to be separated from any other business in a manner satisfactory to the general manager. When reviewing the proposed separation method, the general manager will review: This change provides more flexibility in how liquor retail licensees are separated from other businesses. The updates can be found in the: If you plan to change the way your establishment is separated from another business, you must submit an application through theliquor and cannabis licensing portal. Bulletins are for general information and may not apply to all situations. Bulletins do not constitute legal advice nor are they a comprehensive statement of the legal obligations that arise under the Liquor Control and Licensing Act, Cannabis Control and Licensing Act, regulations, or any other applicable laws. When interpreting and applying the information contained in the Bulletins, you are encouraged to seek advice from your professional advisors as appropriate in the circumstances. The B.C. Public Service acknowledges the territories of First Nations around B.C. and is grateful to carry out our work on these lands. We acknowledge the rights, interests, priorities, and concerns of all Indigenous Peoples - First Nations, Métis, and Inuit - respecting and acknowledging their distinct cultures, histories, rights, laws, and governments.

minutes_briefs.title: Liquor stores no longer need floor-to-ceiling walls to separate from other businesses
minutes_briefs.summary: British Columbia has changed the rules for liquor retail stores. Previously, Licensee Retail Stores and Wine Stores had to be separated from other businesses by floor-to-ceiling walls. Now, the separation only needs to be 'satisfactory to the general manager,' giving store owners more flexibility in how they set up their space. The general manager will review each proposed separation method on a case-by-case basis.
minutes_briefs.action_required: If you plan to change how your store is separated from another business, submit an application through the BC liquor and cannabis licensing portal.
